info.title: Lady Butler GPT Inbox
info.description: Send task and calendar event candidates from a Custom GPT conversation to the Lady Butler app. When private sync is configured, candidates arrive directly in the app inbox. Otherwise, the API safely returns an import URL.

post.summary: Send task or calendar candidates to Lady Butler
post.description: Send candidates only after distinguishing them by purpose. Split separate actions into separate items. A task is something the user completes and checks off, including assignments, submissions, errands, purchases, and messages. A task stays type task even when its deadline includes a clock time, such as 'Friday by 18:00'; put that time in deadline and never in startAt. An event is time the user attends or reserves on a calendar, including classes, shifts, appointments, meetings, and travel with a known start time; put that time in startAt. If the user says 'submit my shift preferences', that is a task, while 'work a shift from 18:00' is an event. Do not use a category named 予定. Resolve relative dates from the current conversation date, but never invent an unknown date or time. Ask one short clarification when the type, task deadline date, or required event start is genuinely unclear. If delivery is synced, tell the user it arrived in the Lady Butler inbox. If delivery is link, return importUrl and ask the user to open it once.

sourceText.description: Short quote or paraphrase of what the user said.

items.description: Preferred input. Every candidate must explicitly declare type task or event.

type.description: Use task for a completable/checkable action, even if its deadline has a time. Use event only for calendar time with a known start.

deadline.description: Task only. Deadline in ISO format. A phrase such as '18:00までに' belongs here, not in startAt. If unknown, omit it and mention the uncertainty in memo.

startAt.description: Event only. Known start date/time in ISO-like local format, such as 2026-06-25T14:00. Never use a task deadline here.

endAt.description: For event items only. End date/time in ISO-like local format. If unknown, omit it and the app will use about one hour.

location.description: Event only. Place, room, station, URL, or online location.

category.description: Task only. Do not use 予定; calendar entries must use type event instead.

memo.description: Useful note such as class name, report condition, first step, location details, or uncertainty.

confidence.description: Use high when type and essential date/time are explicit, medium when only optional details are missing, and low only when preserving a user-stated ambiguity. Prefer asking one clarification instead of sending low confidence.

ambiguities.description: Short Japanese notes about unresolved details that the app should show as 要確認. Omit when there is no ambiguity.

events.description: Compatibility input for calendar events only. Each event must include a known startAt. Ask the user when the start time is unknown; never invent it.

importUrl.description: Present only for link delivery. Open this URL once to import candidates into Lady Butler.
